question: Does the generated text start by thanking the players for their support as requested?
Does the text include a section explicitly titled 'Character Three-View Reveal'?
Does the text include a section explicitly titled 'In-Game Combat Preview'?
Does the 'Character Three-View Reveal' section provide a detailed introduction to the design philosophy behind the mecha armor?
Does the 'In-Game Combat Preview' section describe skill effects, specifically mentioning blue sword energy?
Does the output include a generated full-body back-view image of the character?
Does the output include a generated full-body side-view image of the character?
Does the output include a generated Battle CG illustration depicting the character swinging a greatsword?
Does the Battle CG illustration clearly display blue sword energy effects?
Is the background of the Battle CG depicted as a 'ruined battlefield'?
Are all generated images free from severe artifacts (e.g., distorted limbs, melted faces, broken geometry)?
Do the generated back-view and side-view images maintain the character's key features (red ponytail, black-and-white mecha armor) consistent with the input?
Does the character in the Battle CG illustration visually match the identity of the input character (red hair, armor design)?
Does the weapon in the Battle CG maintain visual consistency with the greatsword shown in the input image?
Do all generated images maintain a consistent art style (anime/game concept art) matching the input illustration?
Do the back-view and side-view images provide valid spatial information (rear armor details, profile silhouette) inferred from the front view rather than duplicating it?
Does the Battle CG image visually reflect the skill effects (blue energy, swinging motion) described in the accompanying text?
Are the back-view and side-view images placed within or immediately adjacent to the 'Character Three-View Reveal' section?
Is the Battle CG illustration placed within or immediately adjacent to the 'In-Game Combat Preview' section?
Does the output include at least three generated images (back-view, side-view, and Battle CG) in total?
Does the model successfully infer plausible rear armor details in the back-view image that logically correspond to the front-view design?
Does the Battle CG demonstrate successful generalization from a static pose to a dynamic combat action without distorting the armor's structure?
